topic: An athlete doesn't make the team they expected to join
raw_output:
[story 1]

The silence in the locker room was deafening, a heavy blanket smothering any semblance of camaraderie. I stared at the team roster taped to the wall, my knuckles white where I gripped my duffel bag. They had picked Marcus instead of me. Marcus, the golden boy, the one everyone had predicted would be my ticket. A slow, tight knot began to form in my chest, constricting my breath. When Coach finally called the final names, the air seemed to freeze. I didn't move, just watched the name being announced, a cold, hard feeling settling deep in my gut. I walked out into the hallway, the fluorescent lights buzzing overhead, each step feeling weighted with disappointment, a dull ache radiating through my shoulders.

[story 2]

"Well, that's that," I muttered, the words tasting like ash in my mouth. I didn't bother trying to look casual as I gathered my gear. Every glance from a teammate felt like an accusation, their smiles too bright, their casual conversation a grating noise against my ears. I could feel the heat rising in my neck, a flush of pure irritation that burned beneath my skin. I shoved my hands deep into my pockets, trying to physically contain the churning resentment. *Of course,* I thought bitterly, watching the other players gather their things with an easy confidence I utterly lacked. It was a humiliating deflation, a sharp, unwelcome sting behind my eyes.

[story 3]

The rejection letter sat on my desk, crisp and unforgiving. I reread the rejection email three times, my jaw tightening until my molars ached. It wasn't a shock; it was the inevitable conclusion of every grueling early morning, every sacrifice I’d made. I slammed the letter down onto the wood, the sound echoing sharply in the empty apartment. I paced the small room, my movements jerky and agitated. I picked up my phone, intending to call someone, but stopped myself. The urge to lash out, to scream at the unfairness of it all, was strong, but I forced the energy inward, channeling the furious energy into a rigid stillness. I just stood there, letting the simmering frustration vibrate just beneath the surface of my skin.
